analysis.background: This review addresses hantavirus as an emerging zoonosis with limited data in the Middle East, aiming to synthesize current knowledge.
analysis.main_directions: It outlines the two main clinical syndromes (HFRS and HCPS) and highlights environmental, rodent, and One Health aspects as research foci.
analysis.current_state: Recent studies show low but detectable hantavirus circulation in countries such as Türkiye and Iran, with rare human cases.
analysis.gaps: Surveillance is sparse, cases are underdiagnosed, and clinical presentation overlaps with other febrile illnesses, hampering burden estimation.
analysis.future_research: Expanded One Health surveillance, interdisciplinary collaboration, and ecological studies are recommended to improve early detection and prevention.

analysis.summary_en: Background: This review addresses hantavirus as an emerging zoonosis with limited data in the Middle East, aiming to synthesize current knowledge. Main directions: It outlines the two main clinical syndromes (HFRS and HCPS) and highlights environmental, rodent, and One Health aspects as research foci. Current state: Recent studies show low but detectable hantavirus circulation in countries such as Türkiye and Iran, with rare human cases. Gaps: Surveillance is sparse, cases are underdiagnosed, and clinical presentation overlaps with other febrile illnesses, hampering burden estimation. Future research: Expanded One Health surveillance, interdisciplinary collaboration, and ecological studies are recommended to improve early detection and prevention.

papers.title_zh: 汉坦病毒 和中东公共卫生挑战:主题回顾

analysis_zh.background: 本综述将 汉坦病毒 视为一种新兴的动物源性疾病,因中东地区数据有限,旨在综合当前的认识。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 概述两种主要临床综合征(肾综合征出血热(HFRS)和汉坦病毒心肺综合征(HCPS)),并突出环境、啮齿动物及 One Health 方面作为研究重点。
analysis_zh.current_state: 近期研究显示,在土耳其和伊朗等国,汉坦病毒 的循环水平虽低但可检测,且人类病例罕见。
analysis_zh.gaps: 监测稀疏,病例漏诊,临床表现与其他发热性疾病重叠,导致疾病负担估计受阻。
analysis_zh.future_research: 建议扩大 One Health 监测、加强跨学科合作以及开展生态研究,以提升早期发现和预防能力。

papers.summary_zh: 背景：本综述将 汉坦病毒 视为一种新兴的动物源性疾病,因中东地区数据有限,旨在综合当前的认识。 主要方向：概述两种主要临床综合征(肾综合征出血热(HFRS)和汉坦病毒心肺综合征(HCPS)),并突出环境、啮齿动物及 One Health 方面作为研… 研究现状：近期研究显示,在土耳其和伊朗等国,汉坦病毒 的循环水平虽低但可检测,且人类病例罕见。 不足：监测稀疏,病例漏诊,临床表现与其他发热性疾病重叠,导致疾病负担估计受阻。 后续研究：建议扩大 One Health 监测、加强跨学科合作以及开展生态研究,以提升早期发现和预防能力。

papers.title: First nationwide full-genome characterisation of human-derived Andes virus in Chile: a retrospective genomic epidemiology study.
papers.abstract: BACKGROUND: Andes virus (ANDV) is the only hantavirus known to transmit between humans and causes hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome in Chile and Argentina. In Chile, ANDV genomic diversity remains incompletely characterised. This study aimed to characterise the genetic diversity, geographical structure, and molecular signatures of ANDV using human clinical samples collected over a 13-year period (2011-24). METHODS: We conducted a retrospective genomic epidemiology study of ANDV infections in Chile. Clinical samples from patients with confirmed ANDV, collected between March 9, 2011, and June 27, 2024, were analysed and sequenced. Clinical and epidemiological data were obtained from diagnostic laboratories and surveillance programmes. Consensus sequences for the S, M, and L segments were generated, and genetic clustering and divergence were assessed using phylogenetic inference and variant calling. FINDINGS: We analysed clinical samples from 58 infected individuals and identified two major genomic variants of ANDV with distinct geographical distributions, defined by regionally structured patterns of nucleotide and amino acid substitutions across the S, M, and L segments: ANDV Chi-North (central Chile) and ANDV-South (southern Chile). No consistent clustering by clinical severity was observed, and no recurrent non-synonymous substitutions were uniquely associated with severe disease. Substitutions previously associated with person-to-person transmission in outbreaks in Argentina were not consistently observed in Chilean sequences, including in four person-to-person transmission cases. Although some substitutions described in ANDV-like viruses were present in the Chi-North lineage, this lineage remained phylogenetically distinct and geographically restricted to central Chile. INTERPRETATION: To our knowledge, this study provides the first nationwide genomic characterisation of human-derived ANDV in Chile. The identification of geographically structured variants indicates that ANDV diversity in Chile is driven by regional diversification rather than clinical outcome. The absence of consistent amino acid signatures associated with disease severity or person-to-person transmission suggests that these phenotypes are unlikely to be explained by viral genetic variation alone. These findings refine current understanding of ANDV evolution and highlight the need for continued integrated genomic surveillance in endemic regions. FUNDING: Agencia Nacional de Investigación y Desarrollo de Chile and National Institutes of Health.

analysis.background: Andes virus (ANDV) is the only hantavirus known to transmit between humans and causes hantavirus cardiopulmonary syndrome in Chile and Argentina, yet its genomic diversity in Chile is incompletely characterised (A1, A2, A3).
analysis.methods: A retrospective genomic epidemiology study analysed clinical samples from patients with confirmed ANDV collected between 9 Mar 2011 and 27 Jun 2024, with consensus sequences generated for S, M, and L segments and phylogenetic and variant‑calling analyses performed (A4, A5, A6, A7).
analysis.results: From 58 infected individuals, two major geographically structured genomic variants were identified—ANDV Chi‑North (central Chile) and ANDV‑South (southern Chile)—without consistent clustering by disease severity or person‑to‑person transmission signatures (A8, A9, A10, A11).
analysis.contribution: The study provides the first nationwide full‑genome characterisation of human‑derived ANDV in Chile, showing regional diversification rather than clinical outcome drives viral diversity and suggesting that severity or transmission phenotypes are not explained solely by viral genetics (A12, A13, A14, A15).
analysis.limitations: Only an abstract is available, limiting detail on methodology, statistical analyses, and broader contextual information (not reported in the supplied evidence).

analysis.summary_en: Background: Andes virus uniquely transmits between humans and causes severe disease in Chile/Argentina, but its Chilean genomic diversity is poorly known (A1‑A3). Methods: Researchers retrospectively sequenced S, M, and L segments from 58 confirmed cases collected 2011‑2024 and performed phylogenetic and variant analyses (A4‑A7). Results: Two regionally distinct lineages (Chi‑North and South) were identified, with no consistent links to disease severity or person‑to‑person transmission (A8‑A11). Contribution: This first nationwide full‑genome study reveals regional diversification as the main driver of ANDV diversity and indicates viral genetics alone do not explain clinical outcomes (A12‑A15). Limitations: Only an abstract is provided, restricting insight into detailed methods and analyses (not reported).

papers.title_zh: 人源性安第斯病毒在智利的首次全国全基因组表征:一项回顾性基因组流行病学研究

analysis_zh.background: 安第斯病毒(ANDV)是唯一已知能够在人际间传播并在智利和阿根廷导致汉坦病毒心肺综合征的汉坦病毒,但其在智利的基因组多样性尚未完全表征(A1, A2, A3)。
analysis_zh.methods: 一项回顾性基因组流行病学研究分析了2011年3月9日至2024年6月27日期间收集的经确认的ANDV患者临床样本,针对S、M、L片段生成共识序列,并进行系统发育和变异检测分析(A4, A5, A6, A7)。
analysis_zh.results: 在58例感染者中,鉴定出两种主要的地理结构化基因组变体——ANDV Chi‐North(智利中部)和ANDV‐South(智利南部),且未出现与疾病严重程度或人际传播特征一致的聚类(A8, A9, A10, A11)。
analysis_zh.contribution: 该研究提供了首个在智利范围内对来源于人类的ANDV全基因组的完整表征,显示病毒多样性由地区分化而非临床结果驱动,并提示疾病严重程度或传播表型并非仅由病毒遗传决定(A12, A13, A14, A15)。
analysis_zh.limitations: 仅提供摘要,导致方法学、统计分析及更广泛背景信息的细节受限(未在提供的证据中报告)。

papers.summary_zh: 背景:安第斯病毒(ANDV)是唯一已知能够在人际间传播并在智利和阿根廷导致汉坦病毒心肺综合征的汉坦病... 方法:一项回顾性基因组流行病学研究分析了2011年3月9日至2024年6月27日期间收集的经确认的A... 结果:在58例感染者中,鉴定出两种主要的地理结构化基因组变体——ANDV Chi‐North(智利中... 贡献:该研究提供了首个在智利范围内对来源于人类的ANDV全基因组的完整表征,显示病毒多样性由地区分化... 局限:仅提供摘要,导致方法学、统计分析及更广泛背景信息的细节受限(未在提供的证据中报告)。

papers.title: Andes Virus - A Clinical Review.
papers.abstract: Andes virus (ANDV) is the sole orthohantavirus with documented human-to-human transmission. We summarize the epidemiology and clinical features of ANDV infection and review best practices in clinical management, as based on published expert consensus guidelines, field experience, and clinical trials. We also evaluate currently available and investigational treatments (including the use of antiviral agents), assess emerging monoclonal antibody therapies, and outline prospects for vaccine development. Finally, we discuss important infection prevention and control measures.
papers.journal: The New England journal of medicine

analysis.background: Andes virus (ANDV) is the sole orthohantavirus with documented human-to-human transmission, motivating a focused clinical review.
analysis.main_directions: The review covers epidemiology, clinical features, best‑practice management, and evaluates antiviral, monoclonal antibody, and vaccine approaches.
analysis.current_state: Management relies on expert consensus, limited field experience, and early clinical trials; infection‑prevention and control measures are described.
analysis.gaps: Data on optimal antiviral regimens, monoclonal antibody efficacy, vaccine candidates, and the effectiveness of infection‑control practices are insufficient.
analysis.future_research: Priorities include controlled trials of antivirals and monoclonal antibodies, development and testing of vaccines, and systematic evaluation of prevention and control strategies.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Andes virus is the only orthohantavirus known to transmit between humans. Main directions: The review summarizes epidemiology, clinical presentation, management guidelines, and examines antiviral, monoclonal antibody, and vaccine strategies. Current state: Existing expert consensus and limited clinical trial data guide treatment, while infection‑prevention measures are outlined. Gaps: Evidence on optimal antiviral regimens, monoclonal antibody efficacy, and vaccine candidates remains limited, and infection‑control practices lack robust evaluation. Future research: Controlled trials of antivirals and monoclonal antibodies, development and testing of vaccines, and systematic assessment of prevention measures are needed.

papers.title_zh: 安第斯病毒 - 临床综述。

analysis_zh.background: 安第斯病毒 (ANDV) 是唯一已记录有人际传播的 正汉坦病毒,促使进行针对性的临床综述。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 该综述涵盖流行病学、临床特征、最佳实践管理,并评估抗病毒药物、单克隆抗体和疫苗方案。
analysis_zh.current_state: 管理依赖专家共识、有限的现场经验和早期临床试验;并描述了感染预防和控制措施。
analysis_zh.gaps: 关于最佳抗病毒方案、单克隆抗体疗效、疫苗候选以及感染控制措施有效性的资料不足。
analysis_zh.future_research: 重点包括抗病毒药物和单克隆抗体的对照试验、疫苗的研发与测试,以及预防和控制策略的系统评估。

papers.summary_zh: 背景：安第斯病毒 (ANDV) 是唯一已记录有人际传播的 正汉坦病毒,促使进行针对性的临床综述。 主要方向：该综述涵盖流行病学、临床特征、最佳实践管理,并评估抗病毒药物、单克隆抗体和疫苗方案。 研究现状：管理依赖专家共识、有限的现场经验和早期临床试验;并描述了感染预防和控制措施。 不足：关于最佳抗病毒方案、单克隆抗体疗效、疫苗候选以及感染控制措施有效性的资料不足。 后续研究：重点包括抗病毒药物和单克隆抗体的对照试验、疫苗的研发与测试,以及预防和控制策略的系统评估。

papers.title: Bleeding in haemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome: A systematic review characterising the loss of haemostasis in hantavirus infections.
papers.abstract: BACKGROUND: Haemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS) is caused by hantaviruses and is associated with variable thrombocytopenia, coagulation abnormalities, and bleeding manifestations. While clinical features have been described in multiple studies, no previous systematic review has synthesised evidence on haemostatic dysfunction in HFRS. This review aimed to characterise the clinical and laboratory features of coagulation disturbances in HFRS to inform understanding of disease mechanisms and future research. METHODOLOGY/PRINCIPAL FINDINGS: We systematically searched MEDLINE, PubMed, CINAHL, Web of Science, and Scopus on 12 December 2024 for studies reporting clinical or laboratory evidence of haemostatic dysfunction in confirmed HFRS. The review protocol was prospectively registered on PROSPERO (CRD42024618760). Observational studies and case series meeting predefined inclusion criteria were eligible. Risk of bias was assessed using the Joanna Briggs Institute (JBI) critical appraisal tools and, for one before-after cohort study, the National Institutes of Health (NIH) quality assessment tool. Studies were graded from 1 (lowest) to 3 (highest) based on methodological quality. Due to heterogeneity in study design and outcome reporting, meta-analysis was not feasible. Weighted averages were calculated for laboratory parameters, and data were visualised using bubble and bar plots. Fifty-five studies comprising 7,950 patients were included. Thrombocytopenia was the most consistent abnormality, typically more pronounced in infections with Hantaan (HTNV), Dobrava (DOBV), and Seoul (SEOV) viruses compared with Puumala (PUUV), which was associated with milder disease. Mild prolongation of activated partial thromboplastin time and elevated D-dimer were variably reported. Limited paediatric data suggested milder disease and less pronounced coagulation abnormalities. CONCLUSIONS/SIGNIFICANCE: This review provides the first systematic synthesis of haemostatic dysfunction in HFRS. Findings highlight more severe thrombocytopenia and bleeding in HTNV, DOBV, and SEOV infections compared with PUUV. Variable study quality and incomplete reporting limit firm conclusions, underscoring the need for standardised, prospective studies of coagulation and bleeding outcomes.

analysis.background: HFRS caused by hantaviruses shows variable thrombocytopenia, coagulation abnormalities, and bleeding, yet no systematic synthesis of haemostatic dysfunction existed.
analysis.main_directions: The review collates data on thrombocytopenia severity, modest aPTT prolongation, D‑dimer elevation, and differences among HTNV, DOBV, SEOV versus PUUV, and notes limited paediatric observations.
analysis.current_state: Across 55 studies (≈8,000 patients) thrombocytopenia is the most consistent finding, especially with HTNV, DOBV, SEOV, while coagulation test changes are modest and disease is milder with PUUV.
analysis.gaps: Heterogeneity prevented meta‑analysis, study quality varied, and reporting of coagulation outcomes was incomplete, limiting definitive conclusions.
analysis.future_research: Prospective, standardized investigations of coagulation parameters and bleeding outcomes, including pediatric cohorts and virus‑specific analyses, are required.

analysis.summary_en: Background: HFRS caused by hantaviruses shows variable thrombocytopenia, coagulation abnormalities, and bleeding, yet no systematic synthesis of haemostatic dysfunction existed. Main directions: The review collates data on thrombocytopenia severity, modest aPTT prolongation, D‑dimer elevation, and differences among HTNV, DOBV, SEOV versus PUUV, and notes limited paediatric observations. Current state: Across 55 studies (≈8,000 patients) thrombocytopenia is the most consistent finding, especially with HTNV, DOBV, SEOV, while coagulation test changes are modest and disease is milder with PUUV. Gaps: Heterogeneity prevented meta‑analysis, study quality varied, and reporting of coagulation outcomes was incomplete, limiting definitive conclusions. Future research: Prospective, standardized investigations of coagulation parameters and bleeding outcomes, including pediatric cohorts and virus‑specific analyses, are required.

papers.title_zh: 肾综合征出血热出血:关于汉坦病毒感染中止血功能丧失的系统性综述。

analysis_zh.background: 由汉坦病毒引起的肾综合征出血热表现出可变的血小板减少、凝血异常和出血,但尚未有关于止血功能障碍的系统性综述。
analysis_zh.main_directions: 该综述汇总了血小板减少的严重程度、轻度aPTT延长、D-dimer升高,以及HTNV、DOBV、SEOV与PUUV之间的差异,并指出儿科观察有限。
analysis_zh.current_state: 在55项研究(≈8,000例患者)中,血小板减少是最一致的发现,尤其在HTNV、DOBV、SEOV中;凝血检测变化轻微,PUUV导致的疾病较轻。
analysis_zh.gaps: 异质性阻碍了荟萃分析,研究质量参差不齐,凝血结果报告不完整,限制了明确结论。
analysis_zh.future_research: 需要前瞻性、标准化的凝血参数和出血结局研究,包括儿科队列和病毒特异性分析。

papers.summary_zh: 背景：由汉坦病毒引起的肾综合征出血热表现出可变的血小板减少、凝血异常和出血,但尚未有关于止血功能障碍的系统性综述。 主要方向：该综述汇总了血小板减少的严重程度、轻度aPTT延长、D-dimer升高,以及HTNV、DOBV、SEOV与PUUV之间的差异,并指出儿科观察有… 研究现状：在55项研究(≈8,000例患者)中,血小板减少是最一致的发现,尤其在HTNV、DOBV、SEOV中;凝血检测变化轻微,PUUV导致的疾病较轻。 不足：异质性阻碍了荟萃分析,研究质量参差不齐,凝血结果报告不完整,限制了明确结论。 后续研究：需要前瞻性、标准化的凝血参数和出血结局研究,包括儿科队列和病毒特异性分析。

papers.title: Spatiotemporal correlation analysis between climate change and hantavirus outbreak in Argentina: Quantitative causal inference based on Google Earth Engine and Bayesian spatiotemporal regression
papers.abstract: Objective: The number of confirmed cases of Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome (HPS) in Argentina surged to 101 in the 2025–2026 epidemic season, nearly doubling from the previous season. There was a significant geographical shift in the epicenter of the epidemic from the traditional Patagonian region to the province of Buenos Aires and the NOA region (Salta, Jujuy) in the northwest. This study aims to use the Google Earth Engine (GEE) cloud platform to integrate ERA5 reanalysis climate data and NOAA surface observation data, construct a Bayesian spatiotemporal hierarchy model, and quantitatively analyze the spatiotemporal correlation and causal path between climate anomalies (high temperature, drought, heavy rainfall) and hantavirus outbreaks. Methods: The data of confirmed cases of HPS from 2020 to 2026, released by the Argentine Ministry of Health (SNVS 2.0), WHO, and PAHO, were collected, and the geographical coordinates and time of onset of HPS were extracted. The ERA5-Land hourly dataset (0.1°×0.1° resolution) and NOAA Global Surface Summary of the Day (GSOD) site data were called by GEE to calculate the monthly temperature anomalies (TA), Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI), and Soil Moisture Anomaly (SMA), and frequency of extreme weather events. Bayesian Spatiotemporal Poisson Regression (Bayesian Spatiotemporal Poisson Regression) combined with the INLA (Integrated Nested Laplace Approximation) method was used to estimate the lag effect (lag of 1–6 months) and spatial heterogeneity of climate factors on the onset of HPS. Causal Mediation Analysis was used to identify the causal chains of "climate anomalies→ vegetation productivity→ rodent population expansion→ human exposure". Results: The spatial distribution of HPS cases in the 2025–2026 season showed significant unstable characteristics (Moran's I = 0.42, p < 0.001). Bayesian models showed that SPI (RR = 1.68, 95% CrI: 1.31–2.14) with a lag of 3–4 months (RR = 1.68, 95% CrI: 1.31–2.14) and TA (RR = 1.45, 95% CrI: 1.12–1.87 per 1°C increase) had a significant positive effect on the onset of HPS, and there was a synergistic interaction between the two (RERI = 0.79). Spatial regression revealed that the HPS risk in Buenos Aires was mainly associated with flood events after extreme rainfall (2.3-fold higher risk in flood months after SPI > 1.5), while outbreaks in NOA were closely related to sudden heavy rainfall ("drought-flood" compound extreme events) after persistent drought. Causal mediation analysis showed that the vegetation index (NDVI) played a significant mediating effect between climate anomalies and HPS risk (41.2% in Buenos Aires and 56.7% in the NOA region). Conclusion: This study realizes the quantitative causal inference of climate anomalies and hantavirus outbreaks at the national scale in Argentina for the first time, and confirms the causal pathway of the "drought-flood" compound extreme climate event driving rodent population expansion and human exposure risk increase through the trophic cascade. The GEE-based real-time monitoring framework can provide a methodological basis for the establishment of the HPS Climate-Driven Early Warning System in Argentina.
papers.journal: Journal of Public Health and Preventive Medicine

analysis.background: Argentina experienced a sharp increase in confirmed Hantavirus Pulmonary Syndrome (HPS) cases to 101 in the 2025–2026 season, nearly double the previous season, with the epidemic epicenter shifting from Patagonia to Buenos Aires and the NOA region.
analysis.methods: Confirmed HPS cases (2020‑2026) with geographic coordinates and onset dates were combined with ERA5‑Land and NOAA GSOD climate data accessed via Google Earth Engine; monthly temperature anomalies, SPI, and soil moisture were derived. Bayesian spatiotemporal Poisson regression with INLA estimated 1‑6 month lag effects and spatial heterogeneity, and causal mediation analysis evaluated climate‑vegetation‑rodent‑human pathways.
analysis.results: HPS cases showed unstable spatial clustering (Moran's I = 0.42, p < 0.001). SPI (RR = 1.68, 95% CrI 1.31‑2.14) lagged 3‑4 months and temperature increase (RR = 1.45 per °C, 95% CrI 1.12‑1.87) each raised risk, with a synergistic interaction (RERI = 0.79). Flood months after extreme rainfall raised Buenos Aires risk 2.3‑fold; drought‑flood events drove NOA outbreaks. NDVI mediated 41.2% (Buenos Aires) and 56.7% (NOA) of climate‑HPS risk.
analysis.contribution: The study provides the first national‑scale quantitative causal inference linking climate anomalies to hantavirus outbreaks in Argentina and proposes a GEE‑based real‑time monitoring framework for a climate‑driven early warning system.
analysis.limitations: Only the abstract is available; details on sample size, model validation, potential confounders, and external validation are not reported.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Argentina saw HPS cases rise to 101 in 2025‑2026, with the epicenter moving to Buenos Aires and NOA. Methods: Confirmed cases (2020‑2026) were linked to ERA5‑Land and NOAA climate data via GEE; Bayesian spatiotemporal Poisson regression (INLA) assessed lagged climate effects, and causal mediation analysis examined climate‑vegetation‑rodent‑human pathways. Results: Spatial clustering was unstable (Moran's I=0.42, p<0.001); SPI (RR=1.68, 95% CrI 1.31‑2.14) lagged 3‑4 months and temperature (RR=1.45 per °C, 95% CrI 1.12‑1.87) increased risk, with synergism (RERI=0.79); flood months raised Buenos Aires risk 2.3‑fold, drought‑flood events drove NOA outbreaks; NDVI mediated 41.2%‑56.7% of risk. Contribution: First national quantitative causal link between climate and hantavirus, offering a GEE‑based early warning framework. Limitations: Abstract‑only; key methodological details not reported.

papers.title_zh: 气候变化与汉坦病毒在阿根廷的时空相关性分析:基于Google Earth Engine和贝叶斯时空回归的定量因果推断

analysis_zh.background: 阿根廷在2025–2026季节确认的汉坦病毒肺综合征(汉坦病毒肺综合征)病例急剧上升至101例,几乎是前一季的两倍,疫情中心从巴塔哥尼亚转移至布宜诺斯艾利斯及NOA地区。
analysis_zh.methods: 将2020‐2026年确认的汉坦病毒肺综合征(HPS)病例的地理坐标和发病日期与通过Google Earth Engine获取的ERA5‐Land和NOAA GSOD气候数据相结合,计算出月温度异常、SPI和土壤湿度。采用INLA的贝叶斯时空泊松回归估计1‐6个月滞后效应及空间异质性,并通过因果中介分析评估气候‐植被‐啮齿动物‐人类路径。
analysis_zh.contribution: 该研究首次在全国尺度上提供了气候异常与阿根廷汉坦病毒暴发之间的定量因果推断,并提出基于GEE的实时监测框架,用于构建气候驱动的预警系统。
analysis_zh.limitations: 仅提供摘要;未报告样本量、模型验证、潜在混杂因素及外部验证的细节。

papers.title: Ecological Cascades and Future Hantavirus Spillover Risk in a Changing Climate
papers.abstract: Abstract Anthropogenic climate change is reshuffling global biodiversity and accelerating zoonotic spillover at the human-wildlife interface. The 2026 multi-country cruise ship outbreak of Andes virus highlights the unpredictable and globalized threat posed by rodent-borne orthohantaviruses, which cause severe, highly fatal human diseases worldwide. Despite this significant public health risk, macroecological synthesis linking environmental drivers to systemic shifts in hantavirus hazards has been lacking. Here, we implement an integrated ecological and machine learning framework combining 89 unique virus-host associations across 61 reservoir species with mechanistic Force-of-Infection (FOI) modeling to project global spillover hazards under current and future climate scenarios (SSP2-4.5 and SSP5-8.5) for 2041–2060. Our models demonstrate a spatially uneven but geographically extensive expansion of spillover hazards, with approximately 74.9% of modeled land area experiencing an increase in FOI. We identify three distinct high-hazard belts heavily concentrated in already-temperate reservoir ranges: western and central North America, central and northeastern Europe extending into western Russia, and discrete patches within central South America. Crucially, our findings reveal that hantavirus hazards are fundamentally shaped along virus-specific ecological axes. While thermal and precipitation anomalies predominate the transmission dynamics of Old and New World lineages like Sin-Nombre virus and Thailand virus, distinct urban-centric Profiles emerge for Seoul virus and Mamore virus, driven explicitly by fine-scale anthropogenic landscape modifications. These results reveal that climate change will not simply intensify viral spillover uniformly but will fundamentally restructure it across varied climatic and human-dominated environments. Our maps provide a critical baseline for targeted global surveillance and highlight the urgent need to integrate spatial conservation planning with public health preparedness.

analysis.limitations: Only the abstract is provided; detailed data, sample sizes, validation procedures, and full methodological descriptions are not reported in the supplied evidence.

analysis.summary_en: Background: Anthropogenic climate change reshuffles biodiversity and heightens zoonotic spillover risk (A1). Methods: An ecological‑machine‑learning framework linking 89 virus‑host pairs across 61 reservoirs with force‑of‑infection modeling projected spillover under current and future climate scenarios (A4). Results: Approximately 74.9% of land area is projected to see increased FOI, with three temperate high‑hazard belts identified (A5, A6). Contribution: The hazard maps offer a critical baseline for global surveillance and call for integrating conservation planning with health preparedness (A10). Limitations: Only the abstract is available; detailed methods, data, and validation are not reported in the supplied evidence.

papers.title_zh: 生态级联与变化气候下未来 汉坦病毒 溢出风险

analysis_zh.background: 人为气候变化正在重塑全球生物多样性,并加速人类与野生动物交界处的动物源性溢出 (A1)。
analysis_zh.methods: 作者采用了一个综合生态与机器学习框架,将61种储存宿主的89个病毒‐宿主关联与机制性感染强度(FOI)模型相结合,预测在当前及未来气候情景(SSP2‐4.5 与 SSP5‐8.5)下 2041‐2060 年的溢出风险 (A4)。
analysis_zh.results: 模型预测约 74.9% 的全球陆地区域将出现感染强度增加 (A5),并在温带储存宿主分布区识别出三条高危带 (A6)。
analysis_zh.contribution: 生成的风险图为有针对性的全球监测提供基线,并强调需将空间保护规划与公共卫生准备相结合 (A10)。
analysis_zh.limitations: 仅提供了摘要;详细数据、样本量、验证程序以及完整的方法学描述均未在所提供的证据中报告。

papers.title: Neurological Manifestations of Hantavirus Infection: A Review.
papers.abstract: Importance Hantaviruses have caused several recent human disease outbreaks. They carry a high mortality rate, and some species can spread from person to person although they are typically spread from rodents to humans. Neurological complications have been documented but are poorly understood. Observations Broadly, 2 types of syndromes have been associated with hantaviruses. The pulmonary syndrome carries a high mortality rate and can result in pulmonary and cardiac failure. Neurological involvement is typically secondary to hypoxic and ischemic injury or metabolic dysfunction. Another manifestation of the infection that is associated with some species of the virus is termed hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome. Some patients may develop a meningoencephalitis or pituitary apoplexy resulting in sudden visual loss and pituitary dysfunction. Rarely, seizures, myelitis, and peripheral neuropathy may occur. Although there are no specific antiviral drugs approved for treatment of hantavirus, there are several that showed efficacy in preclinical trials. Management is supportive care and treatment of symptoms. Conclusions and relevance Neurological manifestations of hantavirus infection are uncommon but can be severe. Prospective studies and experimental models are needed to better characterize these manifestations, understand pathophysiology, identify therapeutic targets, and develop guidelines for management.

They carry a high mortality rate, and some species can spread from person to person although they are typically spread from rodents to humans. Neurological complications have been documented but are poorly understood. Observations Broadly, 2 types of syndromes have been associated with hantaviruses. The pulmonary syndrome carries a high mortality rate and can result in pulmonary and cardiac failure. Neurological involvement is typically secondary to hypoxic and ischemic injury or metabolic dysfunction. Another manifestation of the infection that is associated with some species of the virus is termed hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome. Some patients may develop a meningoencephalitis or pituitary apoplexy resulting in sudden visual loss and pituitary dysfunction. Rarely, seizures, myelitis, and peripheral neuropathy may occur. Although there are no specific antiviral drugs approved for treatment of hantavirus, there are several that showed efficacy in preclinical trials. Management is supportive care and treatment of symptoms. Conclusions and relevance Neurological manifestations of hantavirus infection are uncommon but can be severe. Prospective studies and experimental models are needed to better characterize these manifestations, understand pathophysiology, identify therapeutic targets, and develop guidelines for management.

The pulmonary syndrome carries a high mortality rate and can result in pulmonary and cardiac failure. Neurological involvement is typically secondary to hypoxic and ischemic injury or metabolic dysfunction. Another manifestation of the infection that is associated with some species of the virus is termed hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome. Some patients may develop a meningoencephalitis or pituitary apoplexy resulting in sudden visual loss and pituitary dysfunction. Rarely, seizures, myelitis, and peripheral neuropathy may occur. Although there are no specific antiviral drugs approved for treatment of hantavirus, there are several that showed efficacy in preclinical trials. Management is supportive care and treatment of symptoms. Conclusions and relevance Neurological manifestations of hantavirus infection are uncommon but can be severe. Prospective studies and experimental models are needed to better characterize these manifestations, understand pathophysiology, identify therapeutic targets, and develop guidelines for management.

analysis.background: Hantaviruses have caused recent human disease outbreaks and carry a high mortality rate, prompting a review of their neurological effects. (A1, A2)
analysis.main_directions: The review outlines two major clinical syndromes—pulmonary syndrome and hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome—and describes neurological involvement ranging from secondary hypoxic/ischemic injury to primary meningoencephalitis, pituitary apoplexy, seizures, myelitis, and peripheral neuropathy. (A4, A5, A6, A7, A8, A9)
analysis.current_state: Neurological complications are documented but remain poorly understood; they are usually secondary to systemic injury yet can present as severe primary CNS disease, although such manifestations are uncommon. (A3, A6, A8, A9, A12)
analysis.gaps: The pathophysiology of primary neurological disease, the frequency of severe CNS manifestations, and effective antiviral therapies are unclear, and existing data are limited to case observations without systematic studies. (A3, A6, A13)
analysis.future_research: Prospective clinical cohorts and experimental animal models are needed to delineate mechanisms, evaluate candidate antivirals, and develop evidence‑based management guidelines for hantavirus‑related neurological disease. (A13, A10, A11)

analysis.summary_en: Background: Hantaviruses have caused recent outbreaks and high mortality, prompting review of neurological effects. Main directions: Two syndromes (pulmonary and hemorrhagic fever with renal) are described, with neurological involvement ranging from secondary hypoxic injury to primary meningoencephalitis, pituitary apoplexy, seizures, myelitis, and neuropathy. Current state: Neurological complications are documented but poorly understood, usually secondary yet can be severe, and are uncommon. Gaps: Mechanisms of primary CNS disease, incidence of severe manifestations, and effective antivirals remain unclear, with data limited to case reports. Future research: Prospective cohorts and experimental models should clarify pathogenesis, test antivirals, and create management guidelines.

papers.title_zh: 关于汉坦病毒感染的神经系统表现:综述。

analysis_zh.background: 汉坦病毒已导致近期人类疾病暴发,且死亡率高,促使对其神经系统影响进行综述。(A1, A2)
analysis_zh.main_directions: 综述概述了两大临床综合征——肺综合征和肾综合征出血热——并描述了神经系统受累范围,从继发性缺氧/缺血损伤到原发性脑膜脑炎、垂体卒中、癫痫、脊髓炎以及外周神经病变。(A4, A5, A6, A7, A8, A9)
analysis_zh.current_state: 已记录神经系统并发症,但仍缺乏充分了解;它们通常为继发于全身损伤,却也可能表现为严重的原发性中枢神经系统疾病,尽管此类表现罕见。(A3, A6, A8, A9, A12)
analysis_zh.gaps: 原发性神经疾病的发病机制、严重中枢神经系统表现的发生率以及有效的抗病毒治疗均不明确,现有数据仅限于病例观察,缺乏系统性研究。(A3, A6, A13)
analysis_zh.future_research: 需要前瞻性临床队列和实验动物模型以阐明机制、评估候选抗病毒药物,并制定基于证据的 汉坦病毒 相关神经疾病管理指南。(A13, A10, A11)

papers.title: Artificial intelligence for chest imaging in hantavirus pulmonary syndrome: A review of practical considerations for rare-disease imaging.
papers.abstract: Hantavirus pulmonary syndrome (HPS) is an uncommon zoonotic respiratory illness with a high case fatality rate. Recent travel-associated Andes orthohantavirus clusters have renewed interest in early radiographic recognition of the disease outside its usual geographic range. Artificial intelligence (AI) tools developed during the COVID-19 pandemic now inform several radiology workflows, but the application of AI to HPS chest imaging remains largely unexplored. This narrative review summarises the imaging features of HPS across modalities, considers lessons from AI applications in COVID-19 and viral pneumonia imaging, and discusses practical development considerations for HPS-aware imaging AI, including dataset development, label standardisation, realistic comparator inclusion, candidate modelling approaches, external validation, and clinical workflow integration. The review also considers challenges to HPS-aware imaging AI, including data scarcity, low pretest probability in non-endemic settings, overlapping imaging findings, and translational pitfalls from recent AI literature. As HPS is unlikely to support conventional disease-specific model development without coordinated data collection, future work will likely require multi-institutional datasets, prevalence-aware evaluation, and multimodal workflow integration that combines imaging with clinical, laboratory, and epidemiologic context.

Gaps: Data scarcity, low pre‑test probability, overlapping findings, and translational pitfalls limit progress. Future research: Multi‑institutional, prevalence‑aware datasets and multimodal integration of imaging with clinical and epidemiologic data are needed.

analysis_zh.main_directions: 近期与旅行相关的安第斯病毒 正汉坦病毒 簇重新激发了对早期放射学识别的兴趣,综述概述了影像特征、COVID-19 AI 的经验教训,以及针对汉坦病毒肺综合征的影像 AI 的实际开发考虑因素。
analysis_zh.current_state: 在 COVID‐19 大流行期间开发的 AI 工具已支持多项放射学工作流,但其在汉坦病毒肺综合征胸部影像中的应用仍基本未被探索。
analysis_zh.gaps: 主要挑战包括数据稀缺、非流行地区的低检前概率、影像表现重叠,以及近期 AI 文献中识别的转化陷阱。
analysis_zh.future_research: 未来工作可能需要多机构数据集、基于流行率的评估,以及将影像与临床、实验室和流行病学数据相结合的多模态工作流整合。
